content.text:
[Sun 2026-04-19 16:50 UTC] Shape Sort in `repo/` is too hard to use on phones because the drag targets are too small.

Use the git checkout in `repo/` to get this hotfix onto `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix` and push that review branch to the local `origin`.
I’m going to inspect the repo, apply the hotfix on a review branch, and push it to origin.

arguments.command: git status --short --branch && git branch --show-current && git remote -v && ls -la && find . -maxdepth 2 -type f | sed 's#^./##' | sort | head -200

content.text: I found a dirty working tree, so I’m checking whether the hotfix can be applied cleanly without disturbing the existing changes.

arguments.command: git checkout -b review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix && grep -RIn "drag\|target\|shape sort\|shape-sort\|sortable\|touch" public notes README.md playtest || true && sed -n '1,220p' public/index.html

' && sed -n '1,220p' public/games/shape-sort/index.html

content.text: The issue is exactly where expected: the phone hit area is hardcoded at 6px. I’m widening the touch target and checking for any related size constraints.
I’ve got the hotfix. Next I’m committing on the review branch and pushing it to the local origin.

arguments.command: git status --short && git add public/games/shape-sort/game.js public/games/shape-sort/style.css && git commit -m "Fix Shape Sort mobile hit targets" && git push -u origin review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix

content.text:
Done — I created `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix`, applied the mobile hit-target fix, committed it, and pushed it to local `origin`.

Commit:
- `3b94f53` — `Fix Shape Sort mobile hit targets`

Note: I left the unrelated existing working-tree changes alone.
